package fixture

import "context"

// Inner has NO Encode/Write method of its own. Its fields are written inline by
// a parent encoder (mirroring model.Asset / GW_ItemSlotBase). Pass-3 synthesis
// must walk these flat int32 fields and emit two Encode4 writes so the recurse
// from Outer.Encode flattens into the primitive sequence rather than surfacing
// as an unresolved deferred row.
type Inner struct {
	a int32
	b int32
}

// Outer writes a leading byte, then descends into the self-describing Inner
// sub-struct via field m.inner, then a trailing byte. The recurse marker for
// m.inner has no encode method to resolve against — Pass-3 supplies the
// synthesized field-decomposition Calls instead.
type Outer struct {
	inner Inner
}

func (m Outer) Encode(l interface{}, ctx context.Context) func(map[string]interface{}) []byte {
	w := newWriter()
	return func(opts map[string]interface{}) []byte {
		w.WriteByte(0)
		m.inner.Encode(l, ctx)(opts)
		w.WriteByte(1)
		return w.Bytes()
	}
}

// Opaq has NO Encode/Write method AND carries a field whose type Pass-3 cannot
// decompose: a float64 (the Atlas response.Writer never writes raw floats, so
// goPrimitive deliberately refuses to map it). Pass-3 must therefore leave
// Opaq's Calls nil and flag it Opaque — the explicit register boundary — rather
// than synthesizing a guessed layout.
type Opaq struct {
	value float64
}

// OuterOpaque writes a leading byte then descends into the Opaq sub-struct via
// field m.opaque. Because Opaq is opaque, the recurse marker for m.opaque cannot
// be inlined and must surface as the STABLE "opaque type:" deferred row.
type OuterOpaque struct {
	opaque Opaq
}

func (m OuterOpaque) Encode(l interface{}, ctx context.Context) func(map[string]interface{}) []byte {
	w := newWriter()
	return func(opts map[string]interface{}) []byte {
		w.WriteByte(0)
		m.opaque.Encode(l, ctx)(opts)
		return w.Bytes()
	}
}
